seo.pageTitle: Suwon Hwaseong, Folk Village & Starfield Library | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 10-hour small-group day tour from Seoul: UNESCO Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace, Korean Folk Village (Yongin), and Starfield Suwon Library.
seo.title: 수원화성·한국민속촌·스타필드 소그룹 투어 | AtoC Korea
seo.description: 서울 출발 경기도 소그룹 투어: 수원화성(유네스코) → 화성행궁 → 한국민속촌(용인) → 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관. 전 입장료 포함, 10시간.

catalog_card.title: 수원화성·한국민속촌·스타필드 소그룹 투어
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: 수원화성(유네스코 세계문화유산), 화성행궁(한국 최대 행궁), 한국민속촌(용인, 한국 최초·최대 야외 민속박물관), 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관을 소그룹으로 하루에 돌아봅니다. 전 입장료 포함, 공인 가이드.
catalog_card.subtitle: 서울에서 경기도 경유 소그룹 문화 당일 투어 — 유네스코 성곽, 조선 이궁, 전통 민속 마을, 현대 도서관의 네 가지 대조적인 면을 하루에.

price.priceNote: Original price 67 USD on GetYourGuide; AtoC Korea sale price is a flat 10% discount.

hero.tagline: 한국 유일의 완전한 성곽 도시, 민속마을, 그리고 인스타그램 인기 도서관

galleryItems.alt: Section of the 5.7 km UNESCO-listed Suwon Hwaseong Fortress wall winding along a ridgeline with stone bastions and a major gate visible
galleryItems.caption: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress — UNESCO since 1997
galleryItems.location: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, Janganmun-Hwaseomun section, Suwon
galleryItems.atmosphere: Stone walls built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo; sweeping city views; the only remaining walled fortress city in Korea

galleryItems.alt: Hwaseong Haenggung Palace courtyard with traditional Korean wooden buildings, painted eaves, and stone-flagged ground
galleryItems.caption: Hwaseong Haenggung — Korea's largest detached palace
galleryItems.location: Hwaseong Haenggung Palace, inside the fortress, Suwon
galleryItems.atmosphere: Korea's largest haenggung; King Jeongjo's temporary residence; restored from 19th-century fire

galleryItems.alt: Royal-guard reenactment performers in Joseon-era armor lined up in the Haenggung Palace courtyard
galleryItems.caption: Royal guard ceremony at Haenggung
galleryItems.atmosphere: Daily royal-guard reenactment and martial-arts demos; performance times posted at the entrance

galleryItems.alt: Korean Folk Village's traditional thatched-roof houses set along a wooded path with working artisan demonstrations
galleryItems.caption: Korean Folk Village — 270+ traditional houses
galleryItems.atmosphere: 270+ traditional houses across late-Joseon Korea; equestrian shows; major K-drama filming location

galleryItems.alt: Starfield Library Suwon's four-story atrium with 22-meter-tall floor-to-ceiling bookshelves and reading lounges below
galleryItems.caption: Starfield Library Suwon — 22 m bookshelves
galleryItems.atmosphere: Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield, opened January 2024; free entry; vinyl-listening area

itineraryStops.name: 서울 출발
itineraryStops.description: 서울 중심 2개 집결 지점: 홍익대학교역 8번 출구(홍대, 서울 2호선·공항철도) 또는 명동역 2·4번 출구(서울 4호선). 출발 후 경부고속도로를 이용해 수원으로 이동.
highlights: Pickup from Hongik University (Line 2) or Myeongdong (Line 4) ~08:00
1-hour coach south to Suwon via Gyeongbu Expressway
Coach with air conditioning + USB charging
Designed for early arrival at Hwaseong Fortress before tour-group rush
timeUsed: Meet at designated Seoul pickup point (~5 min for boarding)
1-hour coach drive to Suwon (~60 min in light traffic)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: An 09:00 Seoul departure puts the group at Hwaseong Fortress before midday crowds and aligns with Korean Folk Village's afternoon performance schedule (Hanbok wedding ceremony reenactment, equestrian show, Nong-ak farmers' percussion).

smartNotes.tip: Confirm exact pickup time and meeting point at booking — Seoul morning rush hour (07:00-09:00) makes punctuality critical. Bring water, sunscreen, and comfortable shoes for the fortress wall walking.

_poi_meta.note: Operational pickup — Seoul departure; not a sightseeing POI

itineraryStops.name: 수원화성 (유네스코 세계문화유산)
itineraryStops.description: 수원화성은 1997년 유네스코 세계문화유산으로 등재된 한국 유일의 완전한 형태의 조선시대 성곽 도시입니다. 정조 대왕이 1794~1796년에 축성했으며, 공사 전 과정을 담은 10권 분량의 『화성성역의궤』(별도 유네스코 기록유산 등재)가 현존합니다. 전체 성벽 5.7km 중 약 1km 구간(장안문~화서문, 공심돈·봉돈 포함)을 걷습니다. 4대문(장안문·팔달문·화서문·창룡문), 포루·각루·수문 48개 시설물이 보존되어 있습니다. 입장료 포함. 방문 시간: 약 60분.
highlights: **UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1997** — Korea's only walled fortress city with original construction records
Built 1794-1796 in 2 years 9 months under King Jeongjo (22nd Joseon king)
Designed by Jeong Yak-yong using Korean + Chinese + Western fortification science
Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe construction record — UNESCO Memory of the World 2007
5.74 km circumference, 41 strategic military structures
Janganmun north gate — **largest existing royal gate in Korea**
1.2 km guided walk Janganmun → Hwaseomun (highest photo-density section)
Hwahongmun 7-arch sluice gate — most photographed structure
FREE for hanbok-wearers + last Wed of month + seniors 65+
timeUsed: Coach drop-off near Janganmun (~5 min)
Brief group orientation at Janganmun (~5 min)
Walk Janganmun → Hwaseomun via Hwaseo Janggak (~25-30 min)
Photo time + reassembly at vehicle (~10 min)
Optional Hwaseong Yeolcha sightseeing trolley Yeonmudae↔Paldalmun (~30 min one-way; ₩4,000 adult)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The route's UNESCO anchor — Korea's only remaining walled fortress city, built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo. The Janganmun → Hwaseomun walk (~1.2 km) gives the highest density of preserved military structures + the iconic Hwahongmun 7-arch sluice gate in a 75-min stop. Picked over the full 5.74 km loop because the route covers the most photographed and historically rich section while preserving time for the day's three other heritage stops. Hwaseong is also the **mandatory anchor of any Suwon day-trip** because it physically encloses the Haenggung palace and overlooks Nammun Market — meaning a single 4-5-hour Suwon block can deliver UNESCO World Heritage credentials, Joseon royal-residence interiority, and authentic traditional-market street-food without re-positioning the vehicle. The 1.2 km Janganmun → Hwaseomun wall section via Hwaseo Janggak is the highest-density preserved-military-architecture stretch in Korea (4 main gates + 5 secret gates + 4 sluices + Geojunggi-built ramparts coexist within 25-30 minutes walking time).

visitBasics.hours: Wall path 24 hours (no gates); recommended 09:00-18:00 summer / 09:00-17:00 winter; evening hours May-Oct Wed-Sun 18:00-21:30
visitBasics.admission: Adults 1,000 / Youth 700 / Children 500 KRW (FREE: children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok-wearers, last Wed of month). Hwaseong Trolley separate 4,000 KRW
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — wall has steep sections at Seojangdae; stairs at gates; some sections wheelchair-difficult

convenience.restroom: At Janganmun, Hwaseomun, Paldalmun, and Yeonmudae
convenience.parking: Coach drop-off at Janganmun lot; designated coach parking at Yeonmudae

smartNotes.photo: **Hwahongmun (north sluice gate)** with 7 arches over Suwoncheon Stream — the iconic shot, especially in spring (cherry blossoms) and autumn (foliage). **Janganmun (largest existing royal gate in Korea)** at golden hour. **Seojangdae (west command post)** sunset over Paldalsan Mountain.
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor Information Center at Janganmun + Paldalmun + Hwaseong Haenggung Square. English-Korean signage throughout. Hwaseong audio guide rental at Janganmun.
smartNotes.tip: Wear comfortable shoes — wall stones are uneven. Hanbok rental shops at Haenggung Square give FREE fortress + palace admission. **Avoid full 5.74 km loop unless allowing 2.5+ hours** — the Janganmun → Hwaseomun section gives the highest per-meter heritage density.

sources: UNESCO World Heritage Centre — Suwon Hwaseong (Criteria ii/iii inscription, 1997)
Korea Heritage Service / Cultural Heritage Administration (cha.go.kr — Hwaseong Fortress + Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe Memory of the World 2007)
Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Suwon Hwaseong Fortress official listing)
Suwon City Tourism (suwon.go.kr/tour — Hwaseong Yeolcha trolley + admission + opening hours)
Wikipedia (Hwaseong Fortress — Jeong Yak-yong + Geojunggi crane + 5.74 km perimeter + 41 structures)

itineraryStops.name: 화성행궁
itineraryStops.description: 화성행궁은 정조 대왕이 1789~1796년 화성 축성과 함께 건립한 한국 최대 행궁(임시 이궁)입니다. 정조는 아버지 사도세자의 묘(현륭원)를 참배하기 위해 수원에 행차할 때마다 이곳에 머물렀습니다. 한국전쟁과 일제강점기에 소실됐으나 1989~2003년에 복원되었습니다. 봉수당에서 열리는 무예24기 시범 공연(화·목·토·일요일 정기 공연)을 볼 수 있습니다. 입장료 포함. 방문 시간: 약 50분.
highlights: **Korea's largest existing haenggung (detached palace)** — originally 576 rooms
Built 1789-1796 alongside Hwaseong Fortress under King Jeongjo
King Jeongjo made 13 royal pilgrimages here to honor his executed father Crown Prince Sado
**Naknamheon = ONLY original 1796 structure remaining** (others are reconstructions)
**Royal Guard Changing Ceremony Tue-Sun 11:00 AM at Sinpungnu Gate (FREE, ~15 min)**
1795 Royal Procession documented in UNESCO Memory of the World
Filming location for K-drama 'Jewel in the Palace' (2003, 50M viewers)
FREE for hanbok-wearers + seniors 65+ + children ≤6

**Naknamheon (낙남헌) is the ONLY building surviving from the original 1796 construction** — Japanese colonial-era authorities converted it into Police Station offices but did not demolish it; every other Haenggung structure visible today is a post-1996 reconstruction following the Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe (UNESCO Memory of the World since 2007)
timeUsed: 5 min walk from Hwaseong Fortress (Hwaseomun area)
Arrive in time for **11:00 Royal Guard Changing Ceremony** at Sinpungnu (~15 min)
Bongsudang Throne Hall + Naknamheon + Janganheon walk (~30 min)
Photo time + reassembly at vehicle (~15 min)
Optional Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at Sinpungnu Gate (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM, ~15 min, FREE — weather permitting)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Pairs naturally with the fortress wall — the temporary royal residence is INSIDE the same UNESCO site, 5 minutes apart. Korea's largest haenggung (576 rooms originally), with **Naknamheon the only authentic original structure** (the others are post-1996 reconstructions following the Hwaseong Uigwe). Time arrival for the Royal Guard Changing Ceremony (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM) which is FREE and ~15 min — one of the catalog's strongest free cultural-experience hooks. Visiting Haenggung immediately after the fortress walls converts an abstract military-architecture lesson into lived royal-Joseon spatial experience — the same Bongsudang throne hall where King Jeongjo received officials in 1795 also hosted the lavish 60th-birthday banquet for his mother Lady Hyegyeong (depicted in the Banchado court-painting scrolls preserved in the Uigwe). A **Combined Fortress + Haenggung ticket** is sold at 3,500 KRW (vs 2,500 KRW separately) — a tangible value-added anchor for the Suwon stop. The Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at Sinpungnu Gate (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM, ~15 min, FREE) is timed to align with most Suwon-arrival schedules from a Seoul-departing day-trip.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 1,500 / Teen 1,000 / Child 700 KRW. Combined Fortress + Haenggung 3,500 KRW. FREE for hanbok-wearers, seniors 65+, children ≤6
visitBasics.walking: Easy — palace grounds are mostly flat with some steps between courtyards

convenience.restroom: At main entrance, near Bongsudang, and at Haenggung Square
convenience.parking: Hwaseong Haenggung public parking; Haenggung Square paid lots

smartNotes.photo: **Bongsudang Main Hall** — the iconic throne hall shot. **Naknamheon** — the only original 1796 structure (the most historically authentic photo). **Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at Sinpungnu Gate** (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM). Hanbok-wearers in front of Sinpungnu — Suwon's most popular hanbok photo spot.
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor Information Center, restrooms, gift shop. Haenggung Square cafes and hanbok rental (15,000-20,000 KRW for 4 hr). Suwon Tongdak (whole roast chicken) restaurants 5 min walk.
smartNotes.tip: **Time arrival for 11:00 AM Royal Guard Changing Ceremony Tue-Sun (FREE)**. Hanbok rental gives FREE admission to BOTH Fortress and Haenggung. **Naknamheon is THE single most historically important spot** — the only structure not rebuilt, surviving Japanese colonial demolition. Skip Mondays — Royal Guard Changing Ceremony performed Tue-Sun only.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Hwaseong Haenggung official listing)
Cultural Heritage Administration / Korea Heritage Service (cha.go.kr — Hwaseong Haenggung Historic Site No. 478)
Suwon City Tourism (suwon.go.kr/tour — Royal Guard Changing Ceremony + Mu-ye 24-gi schedule)
Visit Suwon CVB (visitsuwon.org — Suwon Pass combined ticket + Haenggung Square facilities)

itineraryStops.name: 한국민속촌 (용인)
itineraryStops.description: 한국민속촌은 용인에 위치한 약 99만㎡(30만 평) 규모의 자연 계곡을 활용한 한국 최초·최대 야외 민속박물관입니다. 조선시대 각 지역 가옥 270여 채, 전통 공방, 민속 공연(농악·줄타기·마상무예 등)이 상설 운영됩니다. 한복 대여(₩10,000~20,000, 현장 별도)를 착용하면 할인 혜택이 있습니다. 입장료 포함. 방문 시간: 약 120분.
highlights: **Korea's first + largest open-air museum** of traditional folk culture (991,735 m² / 245 acres)
260+ traditional houses representing every Korean region + social class
9 active traditional craft workshops (forge, paper, pottery, weaving)
**UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Jultagi (tightrope walking) demonstrations**
Nong-ak farmers' percussion + horseback martial arts + Joseon wedding reenactments
Filming location for Jewel in the Palace (50M viewers), Sungkyunkwan Scandal, Mr. Sunshine
**Last 2 weeks of October = photographic peak** (golden ginkgo + red maples)
Modest amusement-park section included with combo tickets (family-friendly)

**Jultagi (줄타기) tightrope-walking** — performed daily — is **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity since 2011**: the only Korean tightrope tradition where the walker engages in comedic dialogue with a ground-level musician/MC throughout the performance
timeUsed: 30-min drive from Hwaseong Haenggung to Yongin (~30 min)
Group ticketing + entrance orientation (~5 min)
Self-guided village walk + craft workshops (~80 min)
Performance attendance if timed (Nong-ak / Jultagi / wedding reenactment, ~20-30 min)
Reassembly at vehicle (~15 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The day's biggest heritage block (~2 hours of walking) and the most viscerally late-Joseon stop on the route. 260+ houses representing every region and class, working artisans, equestrian shows, traditional wedding ceremonies, and UNESCO Intangible Heritage Jultagi tightrope walking — all of which dramatize the curated abstraction of Hwaseong's military architecture into visceral living-Joseon experience. **30-min drive each way is offset by 2-hour anchor**. Korean Folk Village is the **definitive sageuk K-drama production set in Greater Seoul** — filming credits include 'Jewel in the Palace / Daejanggeum' (2003-04, 50 million domestic viewers across 54 episodes), 'Sungkyunkwan Scandal' (2010), 'Moonlight Drawn by Clouds' (2016), and many others. For Hallyu-driven travelers, this delivers more authenticity-per-hour than visiting individual filming locations scattered across multiple Korean provinces. A free shuttle bus from Suwon Station Visitor Center (~30 min) or direct Bus 5001-1 from Gangnam Station Exit 5 makes this the most operationally seamless K-drama anchor pair with Hwaseong on a single Seoul-departing day.

visitBasics.hours: Mon-Thu 10:00-18:00; Fri-Sun and holidays 10:00-22:00 (winter shorter — Nov-Feb 09:30-17:30)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 37,000 / Teen 32,000 / Child 26,000 KRW (Visit Korea official; combo includes amusement-rides section). Folk Village only ~22,000 KRW adult
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — large grounds (~5-7 km if exploring fully); paths are flat to gently sloping dirt and gravel

convenience.parking: Dedicated lot — passenger cars 2,000 KRW, SUVs/trucks 3,000 KRW; tour-coach lot accommodates groups

smartNotes.photo: **Last two weeks of October peak for golden ginkgo + maples** in the nobleman's compound. The yangban two-story house and the marketplace street are the most visually rich. Hanbok rental + traditional arena makes for the most popular family shots. Jultagi tightrope-walking performance is high-action photo opportunity.
smartNotes.facilities: Traditional street-market stalls sell local snacks (Imsil cheese pizza, railroad hotdogs, Winibini candy). Multiple sit-down restaurants serving Korean village-style menus. Hanbok rental free 30-min trial inside the gate.
smartNotes.tip: **Check the daily English performance timetable at the gate** — Jultagi (UNESCO Intangible Heritage tightrope walking) and Nong-ak farmers' percussion + traditional Joseon wedding reenactment are the can't-miss shows. **Best visit October 20-November 5** for autumn foliage peak. Pair with Hwaseong Fortress for a coherent Joseon-themed day. Allow 2 hours minimum; some visitors easily spend 3-4 hours.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Korean Folk Village official listing)
Korean Folk Village Yongin (koreanfolk.co.kr — operating hours + festival schedule + admission)
UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (Jultagi tightrope walking — 2011 inscription)
Wikipedia (Korean Folk Village — 245 acres / 260 traditional houses / 1974 founding)
Visit Korea KTO Sageuk K-Drama Filming Locations Guide (Daejanggeum/Jewel-in-the-Palace + Sungkyunkwan Scandal)
_poi_meta.note: Reference fix: Phase 1 used poi_key 'korean_folk_village'; KB canonical key is 'korean_folk_village_yongin'.

itineraryStops.name: 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관
itineraryStops.description: 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관은 2024년 1월 26일 개관한 신세계 플래그십 스타필드 수원 내 4층 개방형 도서관입니다. 국내 스타필드 중 최대 규모(약 33만4,000㎡ 연면적)로, 중앙에 솟아오른 거대 서가 구조가 SNS 인증샷 명소입니다. 도서관 자체는 무료 입장. 쇼핑몰 푸드홀에서 점심 또는 간식을 즐길 수 있습니다. 방문 시간: 약 60분.
highlights: **Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall — opened January 26, 2024**
Designed by BCHO Partners (also designers of original COEX Starfield Library 2017)
**4-floor library with 22-meter bookshelves** — among Korea's tallest cylindrical bookshelves
~70,000 books + magazines accessible FREE
Vinyl Record Listening Lounge with curated collection
Cherry blossom LED-petal installation in atrium (March-April)
Eatopia food hall (30+ restaurants), Aquafield water park, Toy Kingdom, CGV/MEGABOX cinema
Free Starfield shuttle from Suwon Station Exit 4 (~10 min)
Mall hours 10:00-22:00 daily
timeUsed: 15-min drive from Yongin / Korean Folk Village
Mall + library walking tour (~45-50 min)
Café break at library or food hall (~15 min)
Reassembly at vehicle
Optional Vinyl LP cafe lounge (5F, ~₩18,500 incl. drink + headphones + LP record-player access; ~30-45 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 'modern Korea' counterweight after three heritage stops (fortress + palace + folk village). Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall opened January 26, 2024; the four-floor library with 22 m bookshelves and vinyl-listening section is the architectural highlight. **All-indoor stop** — buffers any late-afternoon weather risk and provides genuine multi-generational appeal (kids → Toy Kingdom; teens → vinyl + bookshelves; adults → café + shopping). Starfield Library is the **single most Instagram-photographed indoor space in Greater Seoul outside of central Seoul** (per Korean travel-media surveys 2024-25) — the 22m bookshelves (vs Seoul COEX's 13m) span 4 mall floors (4F-7F atrium void), and the rooftop-level **'The Planets' installation by Canadian novelist-designer Douglas Coupland** is exclusive to the Suwon location. **FREE admission, no membership, photos permitted** — making this a no-friction closing photo stop after the more-paid Hwaseong/Haenggung/Folk-Village earlier in the day. From Suwon Station: Subway Line 1 to Hwaseo Station Exit 1/4 (~3 min on subway, 8-min walk), or free Starfield shuttle bus (every 10-15 min from Suwon Station Exit 4).

visitBasics.hours: Mall 10:00-22:00; library accessible during all mall hours
visitBasics.closed: 1-2 monthly closure days (typically Mondays — verify online before visit)
visitBasics.admission: **Library FREE** (mall-wide free entry; pay-as-you-go for shopping; Aquafield water park separate ~25,000-35,000 KRW)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — escalators and elevators between all floors; wheelchair/stroller accessible throughout

convenience.restroom: Multiple restrooms on every floor; family-friendly with diaper-changing stations
convenience.parking: Underground parking ~5,000 spaces; first 3 hours FREE with mall purchase 30,000 KRW+; coach lot for groups

smartNotes.photo: **Library central atrium with 22-meter bookshelves** — the iconic shot, best from 4F gallery looking down. **Vinyl Record Lounge** for retro aesthetic. **Cherry blossom installation in atrium (March-April)** — viral seasonal photo. Christmas tree (December). Children's section on 4F for family photos.
smartNotes.facilities: Eatopia food hall B1F (30+ restaurants 5,000-40,000 KRW), Toy Kingdom 4F, Aquafield water park B1-2F, T-Joy + CGV + MEGABOX cinema, free WiFi, multilingual customer service, tax-refund counters at major stores.
smartNotes.tip: **Library is FREE** — exceptional value attraction. **Free Starfield shuttle from Suwon Station Exit 4** is the easiest access (every 10-15 min). **22-meter bookshelves = Korea's tallest library bookshelves** (mention to guests for impact). **Cherry blossom installation March-April** is the seasonal must-see. Avoid Saturday afternoons (peak crowd density). 1-2 monthly closure days — verify online before visit.

sources: Starfield Suwon official (starfield.co.kr/suwon — opening date 2024-01-26 + library 4F-7F + free admission)
Shinsegae Property press release (Jan 2024 grand opening + 5th Starfield mall + 2nd-generation flagship concept)
Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Starfield Library Suwon listing)
ArchDaily — BCHO Partners (Cho Byoung-soo) Starfield Suwon design review
Wikipedia (Starfield Suwon — 22m bookshelves + Douglas Coupland 'The Planets' rooftop installation)

itineraryStops.name: 서울 귀환
itineraryStops.description: 서울 중심 집결 지점(홍익대학교역 8번 출구 또는 명동역)으로 귀환. 도착 시각 18:00~18:30(교통 상황에 따라 변동).
highlights: Coach return via Gyeongbu Expressway
Drop-off at Hongik University or Myeongdong (same as morning pickup)
Typical arrival 18:00-18:30
timeUsed: Coach drive from Suwon to Seoul (~60 min)
Drop-off at designated point
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — drop-off chosen for guest convenience and timing certainty. Central Seoul drop-off in the early evening preserves time for independent dinner planning.

smartNotes.tip: Final arrival depends on Friday-evening / weekend traffic; on heavy days the drop-off may add 15-20 min.

routePhases.description: 수원화성 성벽과 화성행궁 — 도보 이동 거리 내에 모두 위치.
30분 이동 후 용인 한국민속촌 야외 공원 탐방.
수원 스타필드로 이동해 별마당도서관 관람 및 푸드홀 점심.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A compact triangle south of Seoul — Suwon's UNESCO fortress city, Yongin's open-air Joseon village, and Suwon's flagship modern mall — that adds up to a four-stop survey of Korean public space across 250 years. Walls in the morning, thatched roofs at midday, glass and books in the late afternoon.

bestFor: First-time Korea visitors who want one UNESCO World Heritage site (Hwaseong) plus one open-air heritage village in a single day
K-drama and history-leaning travelers who'll recognize the Folk Village's filming locations from major historical series
Photography travelers — the Starfield Library's four-floor atrium with 22 m shelves is one of Korea's most photographed contemporary spaces
Couples and adult families who can handle ≈4-5 km of walking across the day at a guided pace
Travelers who prefer Gyeonggi Province's heritage range over staying inside Seoul's central palaces
lessIdealFor: Travelers with limited mobility — the fortress wall has steep sections, stairs at gates, and uneven paving
Stroller-heavy groups (Folk Village paths are mostly flat but the wall is not stroller-friendly)
Anyone needing a tight same-day flight buffer (Seoul return ≈ 18:00 with traffic risk)
Visitors who want to see Seoul's central palaces (Gyeongbokgung, Changdeokgung) — this trip stays in Gyeonggi
Mall-shopping-focused travelers — Starfield Suwon is here for the library, not retail (food hall + browsing only, ≈60-90 min budget)

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: UNESCO + folk village + library, in one day
items.detail: These are three of Gyeonggi's most-visited day-trip targets, and they're each awkward to reach by bus and subway from Seoul. The tour combines them into a single coach loop with admissions included, saving you the planning and the transfers.

items.label: More substantive than the standard 'fortress + library' run
items.detail: Many Suwon tours skip either Haenggung Palace or the Folk Village. This one keeps both, which is what makes the Joseon-era story actually land — you see the wall, the king's residence inside it, and the village life it was built to govern.

items.label: Free admission, paid context
items.detail: Starfield Library is free to enter on your own — but it's much more rewarding with a guide who can explain why a mall built it (Shinsegae's MZ-generation strategy), how it differs from the COEX original, and where the best photo angles are without disturbing readers.

items.label: Year-round route
items.detail: Unlike Nami Island or Seoraksan trips that swing strongly with seasons, this itinerary works in any weather. The fortress, the palace, and the folk village all run in winter; the library is indoors. Good fallback when foliage or cherry-blossom timing isn't on your side.

routeLogicSections.title: How the walking load actually feels

items.label: Fortress wall: the day's hardest section
items.detail: The 1 km walked between Janganmun and Hwaseomun has steep sections and stairs at gates. Comfortable shoes essential. Wheelchair-difficult on this stretch.

items.label: Folk Village: ≈2 hours, mostly flat
items.detail: The grounds are large but paths are flat to gently sloping. Plan ≈2 hours of walking; sit-down performance breaks help. Stroller-friendly.

items.label: Haenggung & Starfield: easy
items.detail: Palace grounds are flat with some courtyard steps. Starfield Library is escalator/elevator accessible across all four floors.

items.label: Cherry blossoms early April
items.detail: The fortress walls are lined with cherry trees — early April is the peak photo window. Hanbok rental at the palace makes the day's best photos.

items.label: Summer heat July-August
items.detail: The fortress wall has minimal shade. The day shifts toward Folk Village shaded paths and indoor Starfield time. Hydration essential.

items.label: Winter cold December-February
items.detail: The wall walk is shorter on cold days; Folk Village indoor performance schedules thin out. Starfield is the natural longer stop.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Suwon Hwaseong…
content: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Suwon Hwaseong Fortress admission, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace admission, Korean Folk Village admission.
Not included: meals, drinks, optional folk-village experiences (hanbok rental, archery), tips.
Starfield Library entry is free.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈08:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4) at ≈08:30.
content: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈08:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4) at ≈08:30.
Arrive 10 minutes early.
There is no hotel pickup; meeting points only.
Latecomers cannot be accommodated.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
content: Moderate.
The fortress wall walk involves uphill stone steps and uneven surfaces — about 1 km of the 5.7 km loop is typically covered.
The Folk Village is a large open-air site (≈245 acres) but flat.
Starfield Library involves stairs and escalators between floors.
Comfortable walking shoes essential.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Comfortable closed-toe walking shoes, sunhat in summer, layered clothing in…
content: Comfortable closed-toe walking shoes, sunhat in summer, layered clothing in spring/autumn, warm coat in winter (the fortress walls are exposed to wind).
Camera, water, light snacks.
Cash for lunch and any optional folk-village add-ons.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts.
content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts.
Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Children are welcome — the folk village in particular is family-friendly, with horse…
content: Children are welcome — the folk village in particular is family-friendly, with horse shows and traditional games.
Strollers can navigate Haenggung and the folk village's main paths.
The fortress wall walk has stairs and is not stroller-friendly.
Limited accessibility for wheelchair users on fortress sections.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Suwon and Yongin track Seoul's weather closely (about 30 km south). Plan for hot, humid summers and cold, dry winters; spring and autumn are the most comfortable.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms along the fortress wall in early–mid April.
Hot and humid; bring sun protection and water.
Best season — clear skies, mild temperatures.
Cold winds on the fortress wall; library is indoors and warm.

seasonalVariations.description: Cherry blossoms along the Hwaseong wall and at the folk village; major Suwon Hwaseong Cultural Festival in early autumn.
Folk-village water festival; evening lighting at Haenggung on selected nights.
Maple foliage along the fortress wall, peak Korean Folk Village season for traditional weddings and harvest demos.
Snow on the fortress wall is one of Korea's classic photographs; folk village hosts winter customs (kimjang, ondol fires).

bookingTrustItems.title: 공인 현지 운영사
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.title: 코스 전문
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop
bookingTrustItems.body: 화성행궁 화요·목·토·일요일 무예24기 시범 공연 시각에 맞게 도착 시간을 조율하는 코스 전문가들이 운영합니다.

bookingTrustItems.title: 그룹 규모 관리
bookingTrustItems.body: 한국민속촌은 서울 근교에서 한복 체험이 실질적으로 가능한 유일한 장소로, 소그룹으로 진행할 때 가장 여유롭게 즐길 수 있습니다.

bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact
bookingSupportSteps.description: 출발 전날 저녁 최종 집결 시각 확인.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 당일 코스 안내
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions
bookingSupportSteps.description: 날씨 및 코스 안내 아침 메시지 전달.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 경유지별 심층 안내
bookingSupportSteps.description: 정조 대왕의 화성 축성 배경, 민속촌 전통 가옥 유형, 스타필드 건축 설계 등 가이드 심층 설명.

staticQuestions.question: 입장료가 포함되어 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 수원화성·화성행궁·한국민속촌 입장료가 모두 포함되어 있습니다. 스타필드 별마당도서관은 무료 입장입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 점심 식사가 포함되어 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 포함되지 않습니다. 스타필드 수원 푸드홀(다양한 메뉴, ₩10,000~20,000)이나 수원 남문 시장 근처 현지 식당에서 자유롭게 즐기시면 됩니다.

staticQuestions.question: 성벽을 얼마나 걷나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 전체 5.7km 중 약 1km를 걷습니다. 주로 장안문~화서문 구간으로 공심돈과 봉돈을 포함합니다. 경사와 계단이 있습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 스타필드 별마당도서관은 한 시간이 아깝지 않나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 첫 방문자에게는 충분한 시간입니다. 건축적으로도 인상적이며, 점심 또는 간식·쇼핑을 즐길 수 있습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 민속촌에서 한복을 빌릴 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 현장에서 한복 대여가 가능합니다(₩10,000~20,000, 1~2시간). 한복 착용 시 일부 구역 입장 할인이 제공됩니다.

staticQuestions.question: 수원+와우정사 투어와 어떻게 다른가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 이 투어는 한국민속촌(주요 문화유산)이 추가되며 현대 경유지로 스타필드 도서관을 사용합니다. 와우정사 투어는 사찰 방문을 포함하며 더 조용한 분위기입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 수원·용인을 처음 방문하는 분에게 좋은 선택인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 유네스코 유적지(화성), 문화유산 마을(민속촌), 현대 공간(별마당도서관)을 하루에 균형 있게 볼 수 있는 투어입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 도보 이동이 힘들지 않나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 활동적인 하루로 총 약 4~5km. 화성 성벽 구간이 가장 체력을 요구합니다(가파른 경사, 계단). 민속촌은 넓은 야외 공원입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 가족 여행에 적합한가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 10시간 일정과 여러 도보 구간을 소화할 수 있는 어린이가 동반하는 가족에게 적합합니다. 민속촌이 가족 하이라이트입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 어르신도 참여할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 계단과 고르지 않은 지형이 있어, 활동적인 어르신께 권장합니다. 성벽 구간이 가장 힘든 곳입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 권장 최소 연령은 몇 세인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 만 10세 이상 추천 — 성벽 고르지 않은 지면 도보, 민속촌 야외 활동, 10시간 일정을 소화할 수 있는 어린이에게 적합합니다.

itineraryStops.name: Seoul departure

1-hour transfer south
timeUsed: Meet driver-guide at Seoul pickup point (typically Hongdae, Myeongdong, or Itaewon)
Brief route overview
Begin 50-minute drive south to Suwon
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: An 09:00 Seoul departure puts the group at Hwaseong Fortress before midday crowds and aligns with Korean Folk Village's afternoon performance schedule.

_poi_meta.note: Operational pickup — Seoul departure; not a sightseeing POI

itineraryStops.name: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress
itineraryStops.description: A guided walk along sections of the 5.7 km UNESCO-listed fortress wall, built 1794–1796 under King Jeongjo as a planned new capital. The fortress combined Korean defensive tradition with Western and Chinese ideas of the period — and is the only remaining walled fortress city in Korea. Highlights include Janganmun (Korea's largest gate ever built), Hwaseomun, command posts, beacon mounds, and bastions that follow the natural ridgeline. Paldalmun (the south gate, where Nammun Market sits) is visible from the wall but typically not walked on this section.
highlights: UNESCO since 1997
Janganmun & Paldalmun gates
Beacon mounds and bastions
Sweeping city views from the wall
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
Wrap-up and reassembly at vehicle
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The route's UNESCO anchor — Korea's only remaining walled fortress city, built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo. The walked section between Janganmun and Hwaseomun (≈1 km) gives the highest density of named features per minute on the day: largest gate, beacon mound, bastions, and command posts. Picked over a fortress drive-by because the wall walk is what most travelers remember.

visitBasics.hours: Wall accessible 24h; recommended 09:00–18:00 (summer) / 09:00–17:00 (winter); evening hours 18:00–21:30 (May–Oct, Wed–Sun)
visitBasics.admission: Fortress wall: 1,000 KRW adult / 700 KRW youth / 500 KRW child (free for children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok wearers, last Wed of month)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to challenging — wall has steep sections, stairs at gates and command posts; some sections wheelchair-difficult

convenience.restroom: Available at major gates and visitor centers
convenience.parking: Available near Janganmun and Hwaseong Haenggung

smartNotes.photo: Janganmun (north gate) and Seojangdae command post offer the best wall-and-city panoramas
smartNotes.facilities: Information centers at main gates; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Trolley (1,500 KRW adult) is good for elderly or limited-mobility visitors; runs Yeonmudae→Hwahongmun→Janganmun→Hwaseomun→Haenggung loop

itineraryStops.name: Hwaseong Haenggung Palace
itineraryStops.description: The temporary royal residence inside the fortress, used by King Jeongjo when he visited his father's tomb. Restored from a 19th-century fire, it's the largest haenggung (detached palace) in Korea. Live performances — royal guard ceremonies, martial arts demos — run on most days and bring the courtyards to life.
highlights: Largest detached palace in Korea
Royal guard reenactment
Martial-arts demonstrations
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
Wrap-up and reassembly at vehicle
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Pairs naturally with the fortress wall — the temporary royal residence is inside the same UNESCO site, 5 minutes apart. Korea's largest haenggung (detached palace), restored from 19th-century fire damage. Live royal-guard ceremonies and martial-arts demos run on most days, which adds movement and audio variety after the quiet wall walk.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 1,500 KRW / Teen 1,000 KRW / Child 700 KRW (free for children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok wearers)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — palace grounds are mostly flat; some steps between courtyards

convenience.restroom: Available at entrance plaza and within palace grounds

smartNotes.photo: Sinpungnu main gate and Bongsudang interior with throne are most photographable; royal guard ceremony makes a great action shot
smartNotes.facilities: 3-storey tourist info center & 3D theater outside front entrance (09:00–18:00); free hanbok rental nearby
smartNotes.tip: Time your visit for the Royal Guard Ceremony (typically Sat 14:00, April–November)

itineraryStops.name: Korean Folk Village (Yongin)
itineraryStops.description: A 30-minute drive to one of Korea's largest open-air heritage parks: 270+ traditional houses representing every region and class of late-Joseon Korea, with working artisans, equestrian shows, traditional weddings, and farmers' music performances. The site is also a major K-drama filming location — the same paths appear in dozens of historical series.
highlights: 270+ traditional houses
Equestrian show
Farmers' music & dance
K-drama filming location
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
Wrap-up and reassembly at vehicle
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The day's biggest heritage block (≈2 hours of walking) and the most viscerally late-Joseon stop on the route. 270+ houses representing every region and class, working artisans, equestrian shows, traditional weddings, and farmers' music. Doubles as a major K-drama filming location — the same paths appear in dozens of historical series. Drives the 'first-time Korea heritage' angle harder than any single Seoul palace.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 32,000 KRW / Teen 26,000 KRW / Child 22,000 KRW (Integrated ticket including amusement rides; Folk Village only ≈22,000 KRW adult)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — large grounds (≈2 hours of walking); paths are flat to gently sloping

smartNotes.photo: Traditional performances at the central plaza (esp. equestrian shows) are highlights; thatched-roof houses photograph well in autumn
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple traditional Korean restaurants on site; gift shops with handicrafts
smartNotes.tip: Performances run on a published schedule — check website to time arrivals around the equestrian show; pair with Hwaseong Fortress for a full Suwon-Yongin day

itineraryStops.name: Starfield Library, Suwon
itineraryStops.description: Inside Starfield Suwon — Shinsegae's flagship 'second-generation' Starfield mall opened in January 2024 — the library spans four floors with bookshelves up to 22 meters tall, a vinyl-record listening area, and curated reading lounges. Bigger and quieter than the original Starfield Library at COEX in Seoul. Free time here is for photos, browsing, vinyl listening, and a casual lunch or coffee in the mall's food hall.
highlights: Four-floor library, 22 m shelves
Vinyl-record listening seats
Mall food hall for late lunch
Free admission (entry to library is free)
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
Wrap-up and reassembly at vehicle
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 'modern Korea' counterweight after three heritage stops. Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall opened January 2024; the four-floor library with 22 m bookshelves and vinyl-listening seats is the day's defining contemporary photo. Bigger and quieter than the original COEX library. Free entry, escalator-accessible — the recovery stop before the Seoul return drive.

visitBasics.hours: Mall 10:00–22:00; library accessible during mall hours
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round (check for occasional Starfield monthly closures)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — escalators and elevators between floors

convenience.parking: Mall parking available (free for first 20 min, paid thereafter)

smartNotes.photo: View from 7F looking down at the library is the iconic shot; lower floors give the dramatic 22m bookshelf perspective
smartNotes.facilities: B2 Traders Wholesale Club, multiple cafés/restaurants, Olive Young, Innisfree, dog-friendly stores
smartNotes.tip: Located near Hwaseo Station (Line 1, exit 1); pet-friendly mall with dog-stroller rentals

itineraryStops.name: Return to Seoul
itineraryStops.description: Coach back to central Seoul, with drop-off at the same morning meeting points. Arrival around 18:00 depending on traffic.
highlights: Drop-off in central Seoul
timeUsed: Operational logistics (pickup/meal/transfer/return)
Brief orientation or break
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — drop-off chosen for guest convenience and timing certainty.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (return); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

routePhases.description: Seoul → Suwon city via Gyeongbu Expressway.
Suwon Hwaseong walls and Haenggung palace, all walking distance from each other.
Drive 30 minutes to Yongin's Korean Folk Village, then explore the open-air park.
Drive back to Suwon for Starfield Library and mall food hall.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A compact triangle south of Seoul — Suwon's UNESCO fortress city, Yongin's open-air Joseon village, and Suwon's flagship modern mall — that adds up to a four-stop survey of Korean public space across 250 years. Walls in the morning, thatched roofs at midday, glass and books in the late afternoon.

bestFor: Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: UNESCO + folk village + library, in one day
items.detail: These are three of Gyeonggi's most-visited day-trip targets, and they're each awkward to reach by bus and subway from Seoul. The tour combines them into a single coach loop with admissions included, saving you the planning and the transfers.

items.label: More substantive than the standard 'fortress + library' run
items.detail: Many Suwon tours skip either Haenggung Palace or the Folk Village. This one keeps both, which is what makes the Joseon-era story actually land — you see the wall, the king's residence inside it, and the village life it was built to govern.

items.label: Free admission, paid context
items.detail: Starfield Library is free to enter on your own — but it's much more rewarding with a guide who can explain why a mall built it (Shinsegae's MZ-generation strategy), how it differs from the COEX original, and where the best photo angles are without disturbing readers.

items.label: Year-round route
items.detail: Unlike Nami Island or Seoraksan trips that swing strongly with seasons, this itinerary works in any weather. The fortress, the palace, and the folk village all run in winter; the library is indoors. Good fallback when foliage or cherry-blossom timing isn't on your side.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Suwon Hwaseong Fortress admission, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace admission, Korean Folk Village admission. Not included: meals, drinks, optional folk-village experiences (hanbok rental, archery), tips. Starfield Library entry is free.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈08:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4) at ≈08:30. Arrive 10 minutes early. There is no hotel pickup; meeting points only. Latecomers cannot be accommodated.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. The fortress wall walk involves uphill stone steps and uneven surfaces — about 1 km of the 5.7 km loop is typically covered. The Folk Village is a large open-air site (≈245 acres) but flat. Starfield Library involves stairs and escalators between floors. Comfortable walking shoes essential.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
practicalAccordionItems.content: Comfortable closed-toe walking shoes, sunhat in summer, layered clothing in spring/autumn, warm coat in winter (the fortress walls are exposed to wind). Camera, water, light snacks. Cash for lunch and any optional folk-village add-ons.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts. Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Children are welcome — the folk village in particular is family-friendly, with horse shows and traditional games. Strollers can navigate Haenggung and the folk village's main paths. The fortress wall walk has stairs and is not stroller-friendly. Limited accessibility for wheelchair users on fortress sections.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Suwon and Yongin track Seoul's weather closely (about 30 km south). Plan for hot, humid summers and cold, dry winters; spring and autumn are the most comfortable.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms along the fortress wall in early–mid April.
Hot and humid; bring sun protection and water.
Best season — clear skies, mild temperatures.
Cold winds on the fortress wall; library is indoors and warm.

seasonalVariations.highlight: Cherry blossoms along the Hwaseong wall and at the folk village; major Suwon Hwaseong Cultural Festival in early autumn.
Folk-village water festival; evening lighting at Haenggung on selected nights.
Maple foliage along the fortress wall, peak Korean Folk Village season for traditional weddings and harvest demos.
Snow on the fortress wall is one of Korea's classic photographs; folk village hosts winter customs (kimjang, ondol fires).

staticQuestions.question: Are admissions included?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace, and Korean Folk Village admission are all included. Starfield Library is free for everyone. Hanbok rental, archery, or other optional folk-village add-ons are at your own cost.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No. Lunch is typically eaten during free time at Starfield Suwon's food hall (large variety, ₩10,000–₩20,000) or at a small Suwon eatery near the fortress (Suwon-style galbi or chicken).

staticQuestions.question: How much of the fortress wall do we walk?
staticQuestions.answer: About 1 km of the 5.7 km loop, typically the section between Janganmun and Hwaseomun, including some bastions and a beacon mound. The full loop takes 3+ hours and isn't covered in this itinerary.

staticQuestions.question: Is Starfield Library worth a full hour?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for first-time visitors — it's an architectural draw on its own. Free time is also for late lunch, coffee, or browsing the Starfield Suwon mall (Korea's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield, opened January 2024).

staticQuestions.question: Can I rent a hanbok at the Folk Village?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes, hanbok rental is available on-site for an extra fee (typically ₩10,000–₩20,000 for 1–2 hours). Wearing hanbok also gets you discounts on traditional-experience activities. The guide can point you to the rental booth.

staticQuestions.question: Is there a difference from the Suwon + Waujeongsa tour?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. This tour adds the Korean Folk Village (a major heritage site) and uses Starfield Library as the modern stop. The Waujeongsa version drops the Folk Village and replaces it with the giant-Buddha temple in Yongin. Choose this one if heritage depth matters more; the Waujeongsa version if Buddhist sculpture matters more.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Suwon & Yongin?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — strong first-time pick for travelers wanting one UNESCO site (Hwaseong Fortress), one heritage village (Korean Folk Village), one Joseon palace (Haenggung), and one contemporary photo space (Starfield Library) in a single day. The format covers four contrasting sides of Korean culture without retreading central Seoul palaces. Geographically the route stays in the Suwon-Yongin corridor.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Active walking day, ≈4-5 km total. Hwaseong wall is the hardest section: 1 km walked between Janganmun and Hwaseomun, with steep gradients, stairs at gates, and uneven historic surfaces. Korean Folk Village adds ≈2 hours of walking on flat-to-gently-sloping paths (270+ houses spread out). Haenggung Palace and Starfield Library are easy indoor/courtyard surfaces. Comfortable shoes essential.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a 10-hour day with several walking-heavy stops. The Folk Village is the family highlight — 270+ traditional houses, equestrian shows, K-drama filming locations, traditional weddings on weekends. Hanbok rental at Haenggung Palace is a fun photo moment (and gets free fortress admission for hanbok wearers). Strollers difficult on the wall, fine at Starfield and Folk Village.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and uneven ground. The fortress wall is the hardest section — steep gradients and stairs make some stretches wheelchair-difficult. Seniors can walk easier wall segments and skip command posts. Folk Village paths are flat to gently sloping, walkable for most. Haenggung is mostly flat. Starfield is fully escalator/elevator-accessible.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up — the day involves significant walking on the fortress wall (uneven surfaces), an active Folk Village exploration, and a long day overall. Younger children can join with carriers but the 1 km wall walk plus the 2-hour Folk Village can tire 6-9 year-olds. Equestrian shows and traditional performances at the Folk Village are crowd-pleasers for older kids.

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.
